rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE A CHORNEY VS. DENNIS L MITCHELL
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 1. DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL's MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Second Amended Complaint is DENIED. Defendant fails to address insufficiency of pleadings in Second Amended Complaint. See Memorandum of Points and Authorities 5:2-9 discussing First Amended Complaint. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE A CHORNEY VS. DENNIS L MITCHELL
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 1. DEFENDANT DENNIS MITCHELL's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff failed to allege outrageous conduct to support a cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE-MARIE YANTES et al VS. VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 5. DEFENDANT VISHNU SHAH's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Shah's Motion to Strike is DENIED. Plaintiffs sufficiently allege facts to support allegations of malice and oppression. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE-MARIE YANTES et al VS. VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 5. DEFENDANT VISHNU SHAH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Shah's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED. Agency allegations have not been challenged and both 5th and 6th causes of action are sufficiently alleged as to "Defendants." = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: TROPHY PROPERTIES V, LLC VS. HUNTER HUGHES et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 2. DEFENDANT HUNTER HUGHES, IV'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's motion is untimely per CCP 1005 and Defendant failed to provide any evidence in support of his motion under CCP 473(d). = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: 1150 WEBSTER STREET, INC. VS. LEON MICKEY LINDSAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 6. DEFENDANT TANYA SAYLES' HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION. Tanya Sayles' Claim of Right to Possession. HEARING REQUIRED. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY ANN MORRIS VS. KEVIN DUNN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 4. PLAINTIFF BETTY MORRIS' Amended Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel responses to written discovery is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide responses without objections no later than January 2, 2012. Defendant shall pay sanctions in the amount of $620 within 10 days. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY ANN MORRIS VS. KEVIN DUNN et al

rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 4. PLAINTIFF BETTY MORRIS' Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Admissions Admitted is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall pay sanctions in the amount of $620 within 10 days. = (SRB/501)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN N. TRAM VS. DAVID SPRINGER
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 28, 2012, to be heard at 10:00AM, line 7. DEFENDANT DAVID SPRINGER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege compliance with or exemption from SFRO 37.9(c). Plaintiff may also address other deficiencies in his complaint. = (SRB/501)
